Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotEffect.Glow

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotEffect.Glow
Enclosing class:
PotEffect
public static class PotEffect.Glow extends Object
Configures a glow effect that creates a colored halo around the edges of an element.

This effect simulates a luminous aura by applying a blurred color outline. The glow's size, color, and transparency can be customized.

Usage Example


 PotEffect.Glow glow = PotEffect.glow()
     .radius(15.0)
     .color(PotColor.CYAN)
     .opacity(0.8);
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

  • Constructor Details

    • Glow

      public Glow()

  • Method Details

    • getRadius

      public double getRadius()
      Returns the current radius of the glow effect.
      Returns:
      the glow radius as a non-negative double value.

    • radius

      public PotEffect.Glow radius(double radius)
      Sets the radius of the glow effect.

      A larger radius produces a wider, more diffuse glow around the element. The value is clamped to be non-negative.

      Parameters:
      radius - the desired glow radius; values less than zero are treated as zero.
      Returns:
      this Glow instance for method chaining.

    • color

      public PotEffect.Glow color(PotColor color)
      Sets the color of the glow effect.

      If the provided color is null, the default color PotColor.YELLOW is used.

      Parameters:
      color - the desired PotColor for the glow.
      Returns:
      this Glow instance for method chaining.

    • getColor

      public PotColor getColor()
      Returns the current color of the glow effect.
      Returns:
      the PotColor instance representing the glow color.

    • opacity

      public PotEffect.Glow opacity(double opacity)
      Sets the overall opacity of the glow effect.

      The opacity controls the transparency of the entire glow. The value is clamped to the range [0, 1].

      Parameters:
      opacity - the desired opacity, between 0.0 (fully transparent) and 1.0 (fully opaque).
      Returns:
      this Glow instance for method chaining.

    • getOpacity

      public double getOpacity()
      Returns the current opacity of the glow effect.
      Returns:
      the opacity as a value between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive.
